questions.question: Explain the difference between endl and \n in C++ with an example showing when each should be used.
keyPoints: endl flushes the output buffer and inserts newline
Use endl when immediate output is needed (debugging, errors)
Use \n for performance in bulk output operations

questions.question: Write a C++ code snippet to display the number 123.456 with 2 decimal places using fixed precision.
keyPoints: Explain that fixed ensures decimal notation

questions.question: What is the purpose of setw() manipulator? How does it differ from width() member function?
keyPoints: setw sets minimum field width for next output only
Both affect only the next insertion operation

questions.question: Explain the difference between cerr, clog, and cout streams in C++.
keyPoints: Use cerr for immediate error messages

questions.question:
What is the output of the following code and why?
cout << setfill('*') << setw(10) << 42 << 99;
keyPoints: Second number (99) uses width 10 again but shows all digits

questions.question: Explain the difference between setprecision(n) with and without fixed manipulator.
keyPoints: Without fixed: n is total significant digits
With fixed: n is digits after decimal point
fixed ensures consistent decimal output format

questions.question: How would you display boolean values as 'true'/'false' instead of 1/0? Write necessary code.
